subject: Anthropology
title: Maritime Archaeology
description: A quick win: understand Maritime Archaeology.

hook.text: A shipwreck off Turkey's coast held Bronze Age cargo: copper, tin, glass, and ivory from four different civilizations.

buildup.text: Maritime archaeology studies submerged sites — shipwrecks, harbors, and sunken settlements preserved by water.

discovery.text: Waterlogged environments preserve wood, rope, and organic materials that decay on land, offering rare details.

twist.text: A single shipwreck can reveal more about ancient trade than an entire land excavation. It's a time capsule at sea.

climax.text: Oceans and rivers were highways, not barriers. Maritime archaeology reveals how water connected the ancient world.

punchline.text: The sea kept what the land forgot.

quiz.question: Why are shipwrecks valuable to archaeologists?
options: They preserve cargo and materials in a sealed context
